step1.title: Welcome, {{name}}!
step1.body: That's your player: name, country, primary and secondary position, age, dominant foot and your OVR — your overall rating. All of it grows with training.

step2.title: Your Energy
step2.body: Energy is your most important resource: you use it to train and to play matches. It regenerates 30% per day, so you can't burn it all at once — take care of it.

step3.title: Your status now
step3.body: You started with R$ 5,000 in the bank and no club — you're a free agent. No contract, no salary yet. To get steady income and play official matches, you need to join a team.

step4.title: Next step: find a club
step4.body: Click here on Club in the left sidebar. At a club you train better (with the team's facilities), get a weekly salary, play official matches and integrate with your teammates. Inside you'll find the button to look for a team that wants you.

step1.title: This is your League!
step1.body:
Here you follow everything about the season:
• Standings — the league table
• Rounds — all matches by round
• Stats — top scorers, assists and more
• Find a club — where we are now, to find you a team

Let's focus on finding a team for you.

step2.title: Clubs that can take you in
step2.body: These are the clubs with a spot for you. Each card is a team. Before signing, it's worth looking at 2 things on each one — I'll show you now.

step3.title: Bot or human? And how many people?
step3.body:
Look at the icon below the name:
🤖 Bot manager — signs your contract right away, guaranteed entry.
👤 Human manager — needs to send you an offer, may take time, but the game is richer.

The human players count tells you how many real people are on the team. More humans = more fun.

step4.title: Let's join
step4.body:
Ready to start?
• Team with bot manager → click Sign contract and you're in.
• Team with human manager → click View club to explore and mark presence.

After signing, come back to Club in the left sidebar — I'll introduce you to your new team! ⚽

step1.title: Welcome to {{clubName}}!
step1.body: This is your team. Up here you see the crest, the name and — below the name — a badge with your manager's type. Each type (defensive / offensive / technical / complete) gives a different bonus to your training.

step2.title: Your manager
step2.body: This is your manager. They decide the lineup, formation and tactics. Treat them well 😄 — and watch their type, because it defines which attributes your training will boost the most.

step3.title: How the team is doing
step3.body:
The club's numbers at a glance:
• Team OVR — average of the 11 starters
• League position + points
• Next match — opponent and time
• Current formation
• Club reputation

Keep an eye here on how the team is performing.

step4.title: Your lineup
step4.body: This is the current lineup. The 11 starters appear with avatar, jersey and position. If you're a starter, your name shows highlighted in blue. The manager picks the lineup — you just check here. Bench and full formation also appear below.

step5.title: Your teammates
step5.body:
Here are all your teammates. Icon 👤 = human, 🤖 = bot. Click any player to see their summary attributes. The more humans on the squad, the more alive the game feels.

Welcome to the team! Let's train and play. ⚽

step1.title: Here are your attributes
step1.body:
This is where you see and train your attributes.

At the top: your energy (no energy = no training) and growth rate — how much you gain per session (drops with age). The History button shows every training you've done.

step2.title: Training bonuses
step2.body:
Each training pays more when you have active bonuses:

• Club manager (defensive / offensive / technical / complete) — bonus on specific attributes
• Training Center at the club — bonus by level
• Private trainer (buy at the Store) — extra bonus

Click to expand and see your combined bonuses.

step3.title: Equipment adds bonus straight on attributes
step3.body:
Boots and gloves from the Store add bonus straight on specific attributes — for example, a shooting boot adds points in Shot Accuracy.

Here you see everything active right now.

step4.title: Colored border = fit with you
step4.body:
Each attribute has a colored border on the left showing how well it fits your archetype + height + position:

🟩 dark green = +50% (perfect fit)
🟢 lime = +25% (good fit)
🟠 orange = −50% (bad fit)
🟥 red = −70% (almost no gain)

Focus on the greens — that's where your training pays off the most.

step5.title: Train here or plan the week
step5.body:
To train right now: click any attribute on the list — opens a menu with the estimated gain, cost (25% energy) and a Train button.

To plan the week: click here on Training Plan in the left sidebar. You schedule 4 sessions per day and the system trains automatically. Let's level up! 💪

step1.title: Plan the week, the system trains by itself
step1.body:
4 slots per day, 7 days a week. In each slot, pick an attribute to train.

The system runs automatically at the scheduled times — you don't need to come back here all the time.

(Energy, bonuses, equipment and colored fit we already covered on the Attributes & Training page — here it's just planning.)

step2.title: Watch out for match day
step2.body:
Match days show a red warning on the day card. Training in the morning burns energy you'll need for the match at night.

Golden rule: save energy for match days.

step3.title: Save + Trainer tip
step3.body:
After planning, click Save — without it, nothing counts.

Tip: at the Store there's the Trainer, who picks and trains 1 slot per day automatically, without you needing to plan the week at all. Ideal for those who don't want to micro-manage.

step1.title: Your full profile
step1.body:
This is your full profile. Avatar, name, positions, age, foot, height, OVR, club, reputation and balance — all here.

There's a button to copy your public profile link — share it on the Forum so managers can see you and invite you to their team.

step2.title: Your look
step2.body:
This is your player's look, set at the beginning of your career. It shows up everywhere: match field, club lineup, forum, sidebar.

The jersey is pulled automatically from the club — no need to configure.

When you buy items at the Store (boots, gloves), they show up here on your look.

step3.title: Averages and history
step3.body:
Your averages per category (Physical, Technical, Mental, Shooting, Defending, Goalkeeping) — quick view of where you're strong.

Right below is the history of your last trainings with the evolution of each attribute. Good for seeing where you're growing the most.

step4.title: Positions and advanced actions
step4.body:
Primary position: 1st change is free, then costs.
Secondary position (right below): paid, but zeroes the penalty when the manager plays you outside your primary.

Further down you also have options to create a second player (parallel career) and — if you're a free agent — reset the current player and start over.

step1.title: Your matches in one place
step1.body:
Here you see all your matches organized:

• Live / Soon — jump straight into the room
• Upcoming — what's scheduled
• Past — with a replay button

Once you have a club and a calendar, they'll show up here.

step2.title: Next league round + presence
step2.body:
When there's an upcoming league match, it shows up highlighted here.

Mark presence to let the manager know you'll play — it's just informative, the manager decides the lineup. But it helps them plan.

step3.title: Want to test now? Run a Test 5v5
step3.body:
Want to try a match right now? Click Test 5v5 up there.

It opens a quick 5×5 against bots in a new tab, no consequences — to show you in practice:

• FIFA-PC style hotkeys (ASDF + Shift for headers)
• Turn phases (positioning → action → resolution)
• Inertia, domination, passes, shots...

Learn by doing. Let's go! ⚽

step1.title: Pickup — casual matches
step1.body:
Here you join random people for a quick 5v5 or 11v11 match.

No responsibility, no energy cost, no XP — just for fun and to practice the game's mechanics. If people don't show up at kickoff, bots fill the teams.

step2.title: Create a lobby
step2.body:
Click Create to open a new lobby — pick format (5v5 or 11v11), kickoff time, side of the field and position. Other online players join the open slots.

Max 3 open lobbies on the server — first come, first served.

step1.title: Your financial health
step1.body:
Overview of your money:

• Balance — what you have in the wallet
• Weekly salary — how much the club pays you
• Current club
• Next payment — always on Monday

No contract = no salary. That's why having a club matters.

step2.title: Contract details
step2.body:
Here are the terms of your contract:

• Weekly salary
• Release clause — the amount another club pays to take you from here (without having to negotiate with your manager)
• Start and end dates

If you're without a club, this card shows a shortcut to go find one.

step3.title: Leave the club
step3.body:
Want to leave the club without waiting for an offer from another one?

Click Request Exit to ask the manager for a mutual agreement. If they accept, you become a free agent right away — no clause to pay, no pending. If they refuse, you stay.

step1.title: Your current status
step1.body: Before accepting any offer, remember what you already have: current club, salary, clause. Compare with what's being offered.

step2.title: Offers that came in
step2.body:
Here you see offers from clubs that want to sign you. Each card shows:

• Weekly salary
• Release clause
• Contract duration
• Squad role the manager offers (starter / bench)
• Optional message from the manager

Buttons: Accept joins the club instantly; Reject discards the offer. No offers? Just wait — or promote yourself on the forum.

step1.title: Your credit line
step1.body:
Ball Dynamics Bank. Here you can take a loan if you need cash fast.

The 3 cards show: Current balance, Weekly salary (your fixed income) and Loan limit (how much the bank lends you — calculated from your income).

step2.title: Take a loan
step2.body:
Drag the slider to pick how much to borrow. Loan details appear in real time:

• Term and interest rate
• Weekly payment deducted from your salary
• Total to pay (principal + interest)

Only take what you need — interest is unforgiving.

step3.title: Pay off early
step3.body:
You have an active loan. Each week pays an automatic installment.

Want to be done with it? Click Pay All to settle the remaining balance and stop paying interest — as long as you have enough cash.

step1.title: Categories and your balance
step1.body:
Game store. Your balance shows in the top right.

Each tab is a category: trainer (auto-training), physio (energy regen), gloves (GK only), boots (raises attribute cap), consumables and cosmetics. Items give bonuses in training, match and look.

step2.title: My Items — what's active
step2.body:
Here you see everything you've bought — active, in the inventory, or canceling renewal.

Trainer / physio / gloves have category exclusivity (only 1 active at a time); boots are exclusive per bonus type. When you buy something that conflicts, the game opens a dialog asking if you want to swap.

step1.title: Welcome to the Forum
step1.body:
This is the game's Forum — where the community talks: players, managers, all together.

Good place to introduce yourself (managers see who speaks well and may invite you), discuss tactics, report bugs, or just have fun.

step2.title: Categories
step2.body:
Categories organize the conversation — general, tactics, transfers, suggestions, bugs, off-topic.

Click any to see only that topic's threads.

step3.title: Create a topic
step3.body:
Click New Topic to start a conversation. Pick the category, write the title and the body.

Tip: paste your public profile link (from the Profile page) so managers can see you in one click.

step1.title: Welcome, manager!
step1.body: This is your club — crest, name, city. Below the name your manager style appears (defensive / offensive / technical / complete). This style gives different bonuses to your squad's training.

step2.title: Club health at a glance
step2.body:
The 4 most important numbers:

• Club reputation — attracts players
• Your reputation as a manager — rises with wins
• Squad size
• Cash on hand — no money, bankruptcy

step3.title: Shortcuts to each area
step3.body:
These cards are summaries with a link — click any to go straight to the full area: Finance, Stadium, Squad, Market, Facilities, League.

All of this is also in the left sidebar when you need to come back.

step4.title: Reports — who's active
step4.body:
Reports shows your players' engagement — who trained, played, bought items. When the yellow warning shows, there's an inactive player — worth releasing them to open a slot for another human.

Let's manage! Next: explore Club in the left sidebar.

step1.title: Club identity
step1.body: This is the club identity page — crest, name, city, uniform colors. Some customizations cost money (R$ 500k to edit), think before spending.

step2.title: Quick shortcuts
step2.body: The cards show summaries of the main areas: Finance, Squad, Stadium, League. Click to go straight to the full area.

step3.title: Uniforms, manager and assistant
step3.body: Below you manage uniforms (home, away, goalkeeper kits), your manager profile and — if you want — set an assistant. The assistant has the same edit access as you (lineup, offers), but cannot fire you.

step1.title: Your squad
step1.body:
Full squad list: OVR, jersey number, name, position, salary, status. At the top you see how many humans are on the team (in green) — more humans = better game.

Click any player to see detailed attributes, offer a new contract, change role (starter/bench) or release.

step2.title: Wage bill
step2.body:
The subtitle shows the total weekly wage. Compare with the club's weekly revenue (in Finance) — wage bill higher than revenue = quick bankruptcy.

Tip: bots earn R$ 250/week. Humans cost more, but they're worth every cent.

step1.title: Formation, tactics and save
step1.body:
Up here you pick the base formation (4-4-2, 4-3-3, 3-5-2, etc), open Situational Tactics (full tactical style) and save any lineup change.

Changing the formation re-organizes the slots on the field below.

step2.title: Lineup, bench and call-ups
step2.body:
Here is your lineup: starters on the field (left), bench and available players (right). Drag players between slots.

Watch out: playing out of position gives an attribute penalty (5% same group, up to 20% distant groups). Secondary position zeros the penalty.

Further down, on Call-ups, humans confirm they'll be present — you decide whether to play them anyway when they didn't confirm.

step3.title: Tactical roles + Assistant
step3.body:
Tactical roles: define the captain and who takes each set piece (right/left corner, throw-in, free-kick).

Right below, Assistant: name another human with edit power equal to yours (lineup, sign, sell) — only can't fire you or dismiss themselves.

step4.title: Uniforms
step4.body: Team uniforms — home, away and goalkeeper. Tweak colors here to change the team's look on the field during matches.

step5.title: Next: Situational Tactics
step5.body:
Lineup is only half the tactic. To control how your team positions in each zone of the field (attack, defense, set-pieces), click Tactics — Situational Game. That's where you draw the team's style.

Click on it now so I can show you.

step1.title: Situational Tactics — the tactical brain
step1.body:
Welcome to the heart of the tactic! Here you define how the team behaves in each zone of the field — without needing to message the players.

Field split into 35 quadrants (5×7). For each quadrant you draw where each player goes.

step2.title: With ball and without ball
step2.body:
Each quadrant has 2 scenarios: WITH BALL (team is attacking) and WITHOUT BALL (defending). You draw a layout for each — totaling 70 micro-tactics.

No need to do everything at once — start with 4-5 main quadrants and improve over time.

step3.title: Visualization and MIRROR
step3.body:
Visualization tools and the most important shortcut:

• Opposite Phase — overlays the opposite phase so you can compare how the team shifts between attack and defense
• Show numbers — each quadrant gets a visible number
• MIRROR — draw one side of the field (right, for instance) and click to mirror; the left side becomes equal and inverted. You only need to design HALF the play.

step4.title: Knobs and quick shortcuts
step4.body:
For quadrants you didn't draw manually, 3 buttons control the whole team:

• Attack type (central / balanced / wide)
• Positioning (compact / spread)
• Team inclination (offensive / defensive)

Adjusting here changes the whole team at once — only doesn't override quadrants you customized.

step5.title: Set Pieces
step5.body:
This is also where you draw set pieces: corner, throw-in, free kick and goal kick — both WITH ball (you take it) and WITHOUT ball (opponent takes).

The system mirrors the side automatically — you draw one side, it works for both. Once saved, every match your team positions exactly as you defined.

step1.title: Player market
step1.body:
List of free agents ready to be signed. Filter by position, archetype, age, OVR.

Click any player to see details and send an offer with salary, release clause, duration and role (starter/bench).

step2.title: Position Demand
step2.body:
Before going around signing, define which positions your club needs. Mark demanded positions here — when a new human creates an account, they see your club as 'wanting' that position and have a higher chance of choosing you.

It's passive recruitment: you attract humans without needing to make an offer.

step3.title: How to negotiate
step3.body:
When sending an offer:

• Higher salary attracts better
• High release clause protects against other clubs poaching
• Longer duration gives stability
• Starter vs bench role influences whether the player accepts

Optional message helps — humans respond more to those who introduce themselves.

step1.title: Weekly revenue
step1.body:
Here you see all the club's income sources: facilities (souvenir, sponsorship), stadium ticket sales, and any extra revenue.

The better the facilities, the higher the revenue. Upgrade via the Facilities menu.

step2.title: Weekly expenses
step2.body:
Player wages, stadium and facilities maintenance, loan interest (if any). Everything goes out automatically every week.

Wages > Revenue = weekly loss → bankruptcy if it persists.

step3.title: Final balance + ticket sales
step3.body:
The final summary shows whether the week closed positive or negative. Ticket history shows how much each home match generated.

Generate positive cash regularly to invest in upgrades and signings.

step1.title: Your stadium
step1.body:
Capacity, quality and prestige of the stadium. Higher capacity = more ticket sales; quality affects revenue per fan; prestige attracts better players.

Upgrade is expensive but pays off long-term.

step2.title: Stadium sectors
step2.body: Each sector (North, South, East, West, VIP) has its own capacity and quality. Upgrade each one separately to balance cost vs revenue.

step3.title: Customize the pitch
step3.body: Here you customize the pitch's look: colors, mowing pattern (stripes, checker, etc), grass tone. Adjust as you wish and save — every home match the team plays on YOUR custom pitch.

step1.title: Club facilities
step1.body: 4 facilities that affect the club: Souvenir Shop (passive revenue), Sponsorship (fixed revenue), Training Center (squad training boost), and Stadium (capacity).

step2.title: Levels 1 to 5
step2.body:
Each facility goes from level 1 to 5. Costs increase: 1→2 R$ 50k, 2→3 R$ 150k, 3→4 R$ 400k, 4→5 R$ 1M.

Prioritize Training Center first (trains your squad faster) and Sponsorship (guaranteed fixed income).

step1.title: Weekly coach boost
step1.body:
Every week you pick ONE boost for the team — tactics, formation, fitness, set pieces, mentality, high press, or training focus.

The boost lasts the whole week and applies to every match and training session.

step2.title: Reset Monday 00:00
step2.body: Once confirmed, it's locked until Monday midnight (BRT) — when it resets, you pick the next one. Think about the style the team will play this week before confirming.

step1.title: Squad engagement
step1.body:
Player list ordered by engagement — days trained × 2 + matches × 5 + store purchases.

The higher the score, the more active. Anyone at zero for weeks is a release candidate to open a slot.

step2.title: Detect inactives
step2.body:
Focus on the reds: players who haven't logged in or trained for a long time. Keeping inactives in the squad locks a slot and wastes salary.

This panel is owner-only — assistant doesn't see it.

step1.title: Invites and friendlies
step1.body:
Here you create friendlies and challenges between clubs. 3 types:

• Solo Lab — you play alone against bots
• Test 5v5 — quick match without consequences
• Friendly vs BOT — full match against a bot team

You can also send invites to other real managers.

step2.title: Next league round
step2.body: If there's an upcoming league match, it shows up highlighted here — direct link to enter the match at kickoff.

step3.title: Received / Sent
step3.body: Track received invites (accept/reject) and sent ones (waiting). A friendly invite becomes a real match when the opponent accepts.

step1.title: Your League
step1.body: Here you follow the whole season. There are 3 main tabs: Standings, Rounds and Stats. Let me show you each one.

step2.title: Standings
step2.body:
The league table. Your club shows up highlighted. Displays points, W/D/L, goals for/against, goal difference.

Good performance here raises your reputation as a manager and attracts better players.

step3.title: Rounds
step3.body:
Click the Rounds tab to see all season matches split by round.

For each finished match you can open the Replay (rewatch the match) and see detailed match stats.

step4.title: Stats
step4.body:
Click the Stats tab to see top scorers, assist leaders and the league's best performers.

Great for tracking who's in form — and who you might try to sign.

step5.title: Available Teams
step5.body:
Since you don't have a club yet, this extra tab shows the Available Teams for you to take over. Click a team, customize name/crest/colors and start managing.

After taking a club, this tab disappears.

step1.title: League schedule vote
step1.body:
Here managers vote on the league match times. You pick 2 preferred days and 1 time.

The winning time is applied next season — so participate to make sure the calendar fits your routine.

step2.title: Real-time results
step2.body: Below you see the current league time and the most-voted option so far. You can change your vote anytime until the vote closes.

step1.title: Club bank
step1.body:
Ball Dynamics Bank — manager edition. Here you take loans in the club's name if you need quick cash.

The cards show: Club balance, Weekly revenue and Loan limit (calculated from revenue).

step2.title: Take a loan
step2.body: Club loans are paid automatically every week, deducted from revenue. Take only what's needed — interest eats cash you could invest in players or facilities.

step1.title: Store — manager edition
step1.body:
Club balance shows at the top. Here you can buy gifts for your players (morale boost) and club cosmetics.

Unlike the player, you spend the club's cash — not your personal.

step2.title: Categories
step2.body: Each tab is a category. Main focus for managers: Gifts (keep humans happy) and club cosmetics. A well-given gift increases human engagement and lowers their chance of leaving.

step1.title: Forum — manager edition
step1.body:
Forum is where the community talks. As a manager, this is where you find humans to sign — a good manager makes themselves seen, talks tactics, proposes partnerships, recognizes good players.

Whoever speaks well here signs better.

step2.title: Categories and topics
step2.body:
Categories organize by theme. To recruit, focus on Transfers and General.

Click New Topic to announce an opening or propose something to the community.
